title: Repo list
blurb: The main two-pane dashboard: repos on the left, live log/diff on the right.

bindings.action: Next repo
Previous repo
Scroll the list view (over the left pane)
bindings.note: web-app style — the selection stays put and may scroll out of view; keyboard nav always keeps the selection on screen

bindings.action: Move the selection (over the left pane), like ↑/↓
Jump to top
Jump to bottom
bindings.note: the Result summary row

bindings.action: Toggle the Result/Errors summary in the preview
bindings.note: any navigation clears it; on a folder/group header: collapse/expand

bindings.action: Toggle the grouped list view
bindings.note: groups from ~/.config/polygit/groups.json; persisted

bindings.action: Toggle the directory-tree view
bindings.note: folders from recursive discovery; persisted; inert when every repo is at the scan root

bindings.action: Fold leader: toggle the selected folder/group
bindings.note: za toggle · zo/zc open/close · zO expand subtree · zM collapse all · zR expand all

bindings.action: Collapse all folders and groups
Expand all folders and groups
Expand the selected subtree recursively
Collapse the selected header, or jump to the enclosing folder/group
bindings.note: tree-style; also the clickable `←/` footer hint

bindings.action: Expand a collapsed folder/group (on its header)
bindings.note: also the clickable `→ fold` footer hint

bindings.action: Refresh dynamic group memberships
bindings.note: command/url sources; re-resolves now, bypassing the cache TTL

bindings.action: Cycle focus across the visible panels: [1] list · [2] info · [3] result · [4] repo page
bindings.note: the focused panel gets a bright rounded border; hidden panels are skipped

bindings.action: Focus the list / info / result / repo-page panel directly — or, when a pane is maximized, swap which pane is maximized
Scroll the result/log panel
bindings.note: when panel [3] is focused

bindings.action: Resume auto-scroll in the preview
Narrow / widen the left pane
bindings.note: also clickable in the status bar; the divider grip fills solid while dragging

bindings.action: Open the dedicated repo page
bindings.note: on a folder/collapsible group header: collapse/expand instead

bindings.action: Retry the selected repo if it failed or was skipped
bindings.note: with a folder/group header selected: retry every failed/skipped repo it covers (folder subtree, recursively)

bindings.action: Retry all repos with an issue
Refetch / pull the selected repo
bindings.note: re-pull regardless of status — incl. idle/cached repos when auto-pull was suppressed; with a folder/group header selected, re-pulls every repo it covers (folder subtree, recursively)

bindings.action: Refetch / pull all repos not in progress
bindings.note: the manual "pull everything" when auto-pull-on-launch is off or suppressed

bindings.action: Refresh the selected repo's local git facts (no pull)
bindings.note: branch, ahead/behind, dirty, stash, branch count — network-free

bindings.action: Refresh all repos' local git facts (no pull)
Toggle the info panel above the log/diff
bindings.note: additive block; tracks the selection. Bold labels; empty rows hidden; clickable branch/commit/remote/pull-request links (branch & remote wrap on separators rather than truncate); the Pull Request row (#N title, via gh) shows when the current branch has an open PR; ⧉ copies the path (and the log, from the log pane); truncated path/subject expand on click

bindings.action: Toggle the result/log panel (the bottom of the preview)
bindings.note: info and result are independent panels in the right pane, split by a draggable boundary; hide the result panel and the info panel fills the pane (reads like the repo list). Persisted; clickable 'I log' footer hint

bindings.action: Cycle the info panel's grouping layout (titled sections → spaced → flat)
bindings.note: or set it in Settings → Layout → Info layout; a maximized info pane always shows titled sections. Persisted

bindings.action: Cycle the result view: log → raw → unified → split
bindings.note: log = pull output; raw = git-colored diff; unified/split = syntax-highlighted. Click any chip in the pane footer to jump directly; the choice persists

bindings.action: Open the columns dropdown
bindings.note: press t (or click the t cols ▾ header chip) to open the columns dropdown, then pick with the mouse, the ↑↓ arrows + enter, or each row's mnemonic letter: u/a/d/l/w/b/s/p/c/r/f → status/ahead-behind/dirty/last/worktrees/branches/stashes/pulled/changed/pull-request/favorite. Toggling a column keeps the dropdown open (multi-toggle); esc/q/the [x]/an outside click closes it. The status column shows a short label with the failure kind (not found / auth / diverged / timeout / network / lock) on failed repos. The pulled (⇣) and changed (±) columns show what the last pull landed — commits and files — hidden until a pull lands a delta, then they stay for the session (a retry won't flicker them). The pull-request column shows a clickable #N (via gh, cached 5 min) for the current branch's open PR. The favorite (★) column shows a clickable star per repo (★ favorited / ☆ not) — click to toggle. Zeros render dim in the Unicode set and are hidden in emoji mode; a column every repo lacks (no worktrees/stashes, ≤1 branch) auto-hides and its dropdown row renders dim + inert

bindings.action: Open the sort dropdown
bindings.note: press s (or click the s sort ⟪col ▲⟫ ▾ header chip, which shows the current sort + direction) to open the sort dropdown, then pick with the mouse, the ↑↓ arrows + enter, or each row's mnemonic letter: n/c/s/a/d/l/w/b/k/p/g → name/branch/status/ahead-behind/dirty/last/worktrees/branches/stashes/pulled/changed. Picking a sort closes the dropdown; re-picking the active sort flips ▲▼ (clicking a column header also flips it). The list is always sorted — Name asc is the default; ties break by name (A→Z), regardless of the column's direction

bindings.action: Status-filter dropdown
bindings.note: opens the status dropdown: a/u/c/s/f/i → all/updated/up-to-date/skipped/failed/issues

bindings.action: Open the selected repo's remote in the browser
Open the selected repo's pull request in polygit's PR viewer
bindings.note: uses the detected open PR (gh); toasts when none is detected. Ctrl+P is the fuzzy finder

bindings.action: Open the PR on GitHub — the existing PR, else the compare page for this branch vs its base
bindings.note: opens the detected PR's url, or `…/compare/<base>...<branch>?expand=1`; no-op when already on the base branch or there's no remote

bindings.action: Copy the selected repo's absolute path
bindings.note: every copy confirms with a toast previewing the first lines of the copied text

bindings.action: Copy the selected repo's remote (origin) URL
Launch the AI coding agent in the selected repo
bindings.note: suspends the TUI; agent (claude/codex/gemini) + skip-permissions set in Settings → Agent; PULL_CLAUDE_CMD overrides the command verbatim

bindings.action: Open lazygit in the selected repo
bindings.note: suspends the TUI; warns if lazygit is not installed

bindings.action: Clear this repo's log buffer
bindings.note: empties the streamed pull output

bindings.action: Open the documentation website in the browser
Open the settings modal
bindings.note: Sections: Lists · Theming · Sync · Interaction · Layout · Tooltips (master toggle + per-area: footer commands, column headers, group counts, settings rows, help links). A radio chip click sets that value (no cycle); the row label cycles. [x]/outside-click closes. Press R (or the R reset footer chip) to reset all settings to defaults — a confirmation lists each change as current → default

bindings.action: Open the keybindings editor (remap shortcuts)
bindings.note: every list / global / repo-page shortcut is remappable; overrides persist to ~/.config/polygit/keybindings.json. Also the [^K remap] button on the help Hotkeys tab. ↑↓ move · ⏎ rebind (press the new key) · c clear · d default · R reset all. Esc/q/Ctrl+C and the new-build Ctrl+R/Ctrl+X stay fixed

bindings.action: Open the Changelog (every release as an accordion, latest two expanded; j/k select, space/enter fold). A What's New modal also pops automatically after reloading into a newer build.
bindings.note: the clickable version tag in the status bar's bottom-right

bindings.action: Open Build info — binary age/size, build duration, settings preview, and pin a version
Open the version picker (pin/switch to any release) — from Build info or the Changelog/What's New dialog
bindings.note: downloads + installs that version and reloads into it; defaults to versions that also have the picker (v2.72.0+) so you can switch again

bindings.action: Version picker: show older versions (pre-picker builds, tagged “no in-app switch”)
Version picker: pin the selected version; or click a release header to expand it (accordion)
Changelog / What's New / version picker: maximize ⇄ restore the modal (or click [m maximize]/[m restore])
Open the help modal
bindings.note: Tab switches tabs (Hotkeys · CLI · Legend · Design · About). On the Design tab, v toggles flat ⇄ tabbed (vertical section tabs) and [/] move between sections; the preview-confirm button opens the shared dialog.

bindings.action: Fuzzy-filter repos by name (subsequence match, ranked by relevance; matched chars underlined; selection follows the first match, Enter keeps it)
Maximize / restore the focused pane (every pane; also the m▢/m▣ top-border button)
Toggle the selected repo as a favorite (★)
Toggle the ★ Favorites section pinned to the top of the list
Open the fuzzy finder overlay — search every repo across all folders and jump to one
bindings.note: type to fuzzy-filter, ↑↓/PgUp/PgDn move, ^S cycles sort (relevance/name/recent/most-used), Enter jumps the list to that repo (recorded in the shared ~/.config/goto-repo/history), Esc closes

bindings.action: Open the folder picker to add a folder (or a single repo) to the workspace
bindings.note: breadcrumbs + ←/Backspace parent, type to fuzzy-filter, Enter opens a folder / selects a git repo, ^S selects the current folder, ^B bookmark, ^H home; the chosen folder is scanned and persisted

bindings.action: Remove the selected repo's (or folder header's) folder from the workspace
bindings.note: drops the root and hides its repos; persisted to the active named workspace (`-w`), session-only otherwise; re-add via the picker

bindings.action: Clear the filter and restore the pre-filter selection, or quit when no filter
Quit
bindings.note: with a modal open it closes the modal instead (the footer hint reads `q close`); `, settings` / `? help` / `q` stay clickable from inside any modal

bindings.action: Quit
bindings.note: exit code 130

bindings.action: Reload into a newly-installed build
bindings.note: only while the top-right `↺ new build installed` notice is up; restores the terminal and exec's the new binary with the same args (also the clickable `[^R reload]`); works from any view or modal

bindings.action: Dismiss the new-build notice
bindings.note: only while the notice is up; re-arms if the binary changes again (also the clickable `[^X]`); works from any view or modal

bindings.action: Open the repo's kebab (⋮) menu
bindings.note: state-aware actions: checkout branch (filterable local+remote picker), copy a cleanup prompt (all repo facts embedded), run the agent, lazygit, diff, refetch, open remote

title: Repo page
blurb: Detail view of one repo (panel [4], restored by default — a docked bottom panel — or maximized full-screen with m): every local branch (with change stats vs the base branch), worktree, and stash, with fresh ahead/behind and a bottom info panel.

bindings.action: Navigate rows
Scroll
Open the diff modal
bindings.note: stash, dirty row, or a branch vs its base; "no changes" toasts instead

bindings.action: Check out the selected branch
bindings.note: clean, non-current branch

bindings.action: Open the columns dropdown
bindings.note: press t (or click the t cols ▾ title-bar chip) to open the columns dropdown, then pick with the mouse, the ↑↓ arrows + enter, or each row's mnemonic letter: b/y/a/m/d/c/u/f/g/r/s → ahead-behind/dirty/added/modified/deleted/total/upstream/base/age/pr/subject (pr = the current branch's open #N, clickable, via gh). Toggling keeps the dropdown open; empty columns render dim + inert; persisted. Stash rows render the same change-stat columns (added/modified/deleted/total), loaded lazily from git stash show

bindings.action: Open the sort dropdown
bindings.note: press s (or click the s sort ⟪col ▲⟫ ▾ title-bar chip) to open the sort dropdown, then pick with the mouse, the ↑↓ arrows + enter, or each row's mnemonic letter: n/b/y/a/m/d/c/u/f/g/s → name/ahead-behind/dirty/added/modified/deleted/total/upstream/base/age/subject. Picking closes the dropdown; re-picking the active sort (or clicking a column header) flips ▲▼

bindings.action: Toggle the bottom info panel
bindings.note: branch/upstream/base + merge-base, ahead-behind, change stats, last commit; persisted

bindings.action: Fast-forward the selected branch/worktree
Fast-forward every fast-forwardable branch
Context action: delete branch / drop stash / remove worktree / discard
bindings.note: dynamic to the row; confirmation scaled to danger

bindings.action: Open the selected branch on the remote (e.g. GitHub) in the browser
Copy menu — absolute path / branch name / both
bindings.note: copying confirms with a toast previewing the copied text

bindings.action: Base-branch picker for the selected branch
bindings.note: overrides which base its diff stats compare against; click the base cell too. Pick "auto-detect" to clear the override; persisted

bindings.action: Launch the AI coding agent in the row's path
Open lazygit in the row's path
Focus the list / info / result / repo-page panel directly (or swap which pane is maximized)
bindings.note: same as the repo list — switches panels without leaving the page; when a pane is maximized, swaps which pane fills the screen

bindings.action: Maximize / restore the repo page
bindings.note: restored = docked panel [4]; maximized = full-screen. Also the ▢/▣ title-bar button (left of [esc back]) and Settings → Layout → Repo page; the state is sticky (persisted)

bindings.action: Back to the repo list

title: Diff modal
blurb: A 90%-of-screen overlay: a file-list panel over the selected file’s diff, in two bordered sub-panels.

bindings.action: Switch focus between the file list and the diff
bindings.note: the focused panel gets a bright border

bindings.action: Drive the focused panel
bindings.note: pick a file, or scroll the diff

bindings.action: Pick the clicked file
First/last file, or diff top/bottom (per focus)
Page the diff panel
Page the file list
bindings.note: Alt too — moves the selection a viewport at a time

bindings.action: Scroll the file list
bindings.note: Alt+wheel too — selection unchanged

bindings.action: Filter the file list by status
bindings.note: shown with >10 files across ≥2 statuses; cycles all → each status → all; clickable chips with count badges, list grouped by status

bindings.action: Toggle uncommitted ⇄ vs base branch
bindings.note: dirty rows only; re-lists files

bindings.action: Discard changes (current branch) / remove worktree / drop stash
bindings.note: with confirmation

bindings.action: Close the modal

title: PR viewer modal
blurb: A centered overlay (diff-modal dimensions) showing a pull request as a GitHub-style tabbed view — a pinned meta header (state, branches, author, +/- stats) over a tab bar: Description · Conversation · Commits · Checks · Files. Loads via gh pr view (one call fills every tab + the badge counts; the Files diff loads lazily via gh pr diff). Opens by clicking a PR link (info panel, repo-page HEAD #N, or a list PRs cell) or pressing p on a repo.

bindings.action: Cycle the tab (Description · Conversation · Commits · Checks · Files)
bindings.note: or 1..5 to jump to a tab directly; click a tab chip too

bindings.action: Jump to a tab by number (Description / Conversation / Commits / Checks / Files)
On the Files tab: cycle the diff render style (raw → unified → split)
bindings.note: or click the raw/unified/split chips; mirrors the diff modal's view toggle

bindings.action: Scroll the PR
Jump to top / bottom
Page up / down
Switch tab, toggle a Conversation comment, or open a check's run
bindings.note: click a tab chip, a ▾/▸ comment header, a Files raw/unified/split chip, or a Checks row's ↗ to open its run in the browser

bindings.action: Collapse all / expand all Conversation comments
bindings.note: Conversation tab; also the [− collapse all] / [+ expand all] control, shown with >1 comment

bindings.action: Search the Description / Conversation
bindings.note: text tabs only; filters to matching comments, highlights hits; Esc clears

bindings.action: Scroll the PR
bindings.note: drag the scrollbar to jump

bindings.action: Open the PR in your browser
Close the modal
bindings.note: the [x] button or a click outside also closes
